activities_projects.title: Case Study on Life Without Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand consequences when democratic rights are denied
steps: Read the examples of Guantanamo Bay, Saudi Arabia and Kosovo
Discuss what rights were denied in each case
Write a short comparison of the three situations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze situations where rights are violated

activities_projects.title: Fundamental Rights Mind Map
activities_projects.objective: Understand connections among different Fundamental Rights
steps: Write 'Fundamental Rights' at the center
Draw branches for each right
Add examples of how each right is applied in daily life

activities_projects.title: Debate on Freedom of Speech
activities_projects.objective: Understand limits of freedom of expression
steps: Divide students into two groups
One group argues that freedom of speech should be unlimited
The other group argues that restrictions are necessary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand balance between freedom and responsibility

activities_projects.title: Right to Equality Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Identify examples of equality and discrimination
steps: Collect examples of discrimination from newspapers
Explain which aspect of Right to Equality is violated
Suggest ways to promote equality

activities_projects.title: Court Procedure Activity
steps: Study the concept of Right to Constitutional Remedies
Write steps involved when a citizen approaches the court
Explain the role of the Supreme Court
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand judicial protection of rights

activities_projects.title: Child Labour Awareness Survey
steps: Conduct a small survey in your locality
Find if any children are working in shops or factories
Write a report explaining why child labour is illegal

activities_projects.title: Religious Freedom Case Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand secularism and freedom of religion
steps: Identify situations where religious freedom is protected
Discuss limits of religious practices
Write a paragraph explaining secularism

activities_projects.title: Minority Rights Project
steps: Choose one minority community in India
Study its language and cultural practices
Explain how the Constitution protects their rights

activities_projects.title: Human Rights Organization Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn about NHRC and human rights protection
steps: Research the National Human Rights Commission
List its major functions
Present findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand institutional protection of rights

activities_projects.title: Rights Violation Report Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop awareness about human rights violations
steps: Identify a human rights violation reported in the news
Explain the rights involved
Suggest solutions to prevent such violations
